description: SPEC §16 test 13 ('old-bl caveat') intends a fixture: a pre-spec bl binary on a workspace with state_url set resolves its OWN git's balls/tasks, not the tracker — exercising the §12 accepted backwards-compat caveat. The shipped tests/conformance_model.rs t13_state_url_routes_to_tracker_not_local_worktree instead asserts the NEW bl routes to the tracker; it exists and fails-then-passes correctly, but never runs an old binary, so the §12 caveat is asserted by prose, not by test. Verify: is asserting the new-side behaviour an adequate stand-in, or should a real fixture be built — pin a pre-bl-8a9a commit, build that bl, run it against a state_url workspace, assert it ignores state_url. Decide, then either document the deviation in the test or build the fixture.
title: Conformance test 13 asserts new-bl behaviour, not the pre-spec-bl fixture SPEC 16.13 intends
